steps.title: Step 1
steps.content: Find a common denominator for $\frac{4}{4x-5} - \frac{1}{x+1}$. The LCD is $(4x-5)(x+1)$.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.content:
• Choice A — $\frac{1}{ \left(x+1\right) \left(4x-5\right)}$: The numerator after combining is 9, not 1. ✗
  • Choice B — $\frac{3}{3x-6}$: The denominator does not factor to $3x-6$; this is not equivalent. ✗
  • Choice C — $-\frac{1}{ \left(x+1\right) \left(4x-5\right)}$: Numerator is positive 9, not −1. ✗
  • Choice D — $\frac{9}{ \left(x+1\right) \left(4x-5\right)}$: Matches the combined expression exactly. ✓
D is correct.
